description_long: This object is a vintage tabletop radio, characterized by a rectangular wooden cabinet with a rich, dark brown finish. The front face features a prominent speaker grille with vertical black slats and a woven tan fabric behind them, giving it a classic Art Deco appearance. Below the grille, there is a circular analog tuning dial with a glass cover, surrounded by a decorative octagonal bezel. Flanking the dial are two round black knobs, likely for tuning and volume control. The sides and back are plain, with the wood grain visible and no additional features. The overall design suggests a sturdy, well-crafted electronic device from the early to mid-20th century, intended for home or office use.
description: A vintage wooden tabletop radio with a front speaker grille, analog tuning dial, and two control knobs. The cabinet is dark brown with a classic, retro design.
description_view_0: The left side of the radio, showing a flat, unadorned wooden surface with visible grain.
description_view_1: The front view displays the speaker grille with vertical slats, woven fabric, tuning dial, and two control knobs.
description_view_2: The right side of the radio, mirroring the left with a plain wooden surface and no visible controls.
description_view_3: The back of the radio, featuring a solid wooden panel with no visible openings or controls.
